architecture.description: Follow layered architecture: Controller → Service → Repository, controllers only handle HTTP, business logic in services
Use DTOs for API requests/responses, never expose entity classes directly to clients

naming_conventions.description: Classes: PascalCase, methods/variables: camelCase, constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE, packages: lowercase
Controllers end with 'Controller', Services with 'Service', Repositories with 'Repository', DTOs with 'DTO' or 'Request'/'Response'

api_layer.description: Use @RestController for REST endpoints, return ResponseEntity with proper HTTP status codes
Validate input using @Valid annotation and proper exception handling with @ControllerAdvice

data_layer.description: Extend JpaRepository for database operations, use @Entity for domain models with proper JPA annotations

infra.description: Use BCryptPasswordEncoder for password hashing, never store plain text passwords
Configure SecurityFilterChain for endpoint protection, use JWT filter for token validation

testing.description: Write unit tests for services using @SpringBootTest, mock dependencies with @MockBean
Test REST endpoints with @WebMvcTest and MockMvc
